sentences.quote: \texttt{RiemannianConnection.lean} ships the Christoffel-quadratic part of the Riemann tensor with an algebraic antisymmetry-in-($\mu,\nu$) proof, the Koszul-formula closed form for Levi-Civita Christoffels, and a torsion-freeness theorem under metric-partial symmetry
sentences.quote_normalized: riemannianconnection.lean ships the christoffel-quadratic part of the riemann tensor with an algebraic antisymmetry-in-($\mu,\nu$) proof, the koszul-formula closed form for levi-civita christoffels, and a torsion-freeness theorem under metric-partial symmetry
sentences.agent_notes: TN-OK: all four targets present.

sentences.quote: \texttt{RiemannCoordinate.lean} ships the full coordinate Riemann tensor including the linear-in-$\partial \Gamma$ piece and the algebraic first Bianchi identity for the full Riemann under torsion-freeness on both the Christoffel and its partial-derivative array
sentences.quote_normalized: riemanncoordinate.lean ships the full coordinate riemann tensor including the linear-in-$\partial \gamma$ piece and the algebraic first bianchi identity for the full riemann under torsion-freeness on both the christoffel and its partial-derivative array
sentences.agent_notes: TN-OK: ChristoffelPartialTorsionFree verified in RiemannCoordinate.lean.

sentences.quote: \texttt{RiemannDifferentialBianchi.lean} ships the second-derivative carrier $\partial^{2} \Gamma$ with a Schwarz-symmetry hypothesis, the covariant-derivative operator $\nabla_\lambda R^\rho{}_{\sigma\mu\nu}$ on a generic Riemann tensor, the linear-piece differential second Bianchi identity under Schwarz alone, and the Group C+D quadratic cyclic cancellation under torsion-freeness plus \texttt{AntisymLastTwo}
sentences.quote_normalized: riemanndifferentialbianchi.lean ships the second-derivative carrier $\partial^{2} \gamma$ with a schwarz-symmetry hypothesis, the covariant-derivative operator $\nabla_\lambda r^\rho{}_{\sigma\mu\nu}$ on a generic riemann tensor, the linear-piece differential second bianchi identity under schwarz alone, and the group c+d quadratic cyclic cancellation under torsion-freeness plus antisymlasttwo

TN-OK: bundleRiemannAux + bundleRiemannAux_self + bundleRiemannAux_swap_apply all live in BundleRiemannAux.lean.

sentences.quote: \texttt{BundleRiemann.lean} ships pointwise cyclic Jacobi for $\mathrm{mlieBracket}$, the abstract bundle-level first Bianchi identity for $V := \mathrm{TangentSpace}\,I$ under torsion-freeness, and root-namespace \texttt{IsCovariantDerivativeOn.neg} / \texttt{.sub} Mathlib-style helpers
sentences.quote_normalized: bundleriemann.lean ships pointwise cyclic jacobi for $mliebracket$, the abstract bundle-level first bianchi identity for $v := tangentspace\,i$ under torsion-freeness, and root-namespace iscovariantderivativeon.neg / .sub mathlib-style helpers
sentences.agent_notes: TN-OK: all four targets verified in BundleRiemann.lean (live grep). Per W8 Session 4a/4b ship memory.

sentences.quote: \texttt{LeviCivita.lean} ships the substantive bundle-level Levi-Civita uniqueness theorem via the Wald \S 3.1 / Kobayashi-Nomizu IV.2.2 Koszul-bilinear-form derivation $2\,g(\nabla_{X}Y, Z) = X g(Y,Z) + Y g(Z,X) - Z g(X,Y) + g([X,Y], Z) - g([X,Z], Y) - g([Y,Z], X)$ from $\mathrm{IsCovariantDerivativeOn} + \mathrm{torsion\text{-}free} + \mathrm{metric\text{-}symmetric} + \mathrm{metric\text{-}compatible}$, plus the algebraic uniqueness kernel routing via bilinearity non-degeneracy and \texttt{FiberBundle.extend}.
sentences.quote_normalized: levicivita.lean ships the substantive bundle-level levi-civita uniqueness theorem via the wald \s 3.1 / kobayashi-nomizu iv.2.2 koszul-bilinear-form derivation $2\,g(\nabla_{x}y, z) = x g(y,z) + y g(z,x) - z g(x,y) + g([x,y], z) - g([x,z], y) - g([y,z], x)$ from $iscovariantderivativeon + \mathrm{torsion-free} + \mathrm{metric-symmetric} + \mathrm{metric-compatible}$, plus the algebraic uniqueness kernel routing via bilinearity non-degeneracy and fiberbundle.extend.

sentences.quote: Cyclic Jacobi follows from one Leibniz instance plus three antisymmetry rewrites: outer swap via \texttt{mlieBracket\_swap\_apply}; inner swap via section-level \texttt{mlieBracket\_swap}; and inner-negation distribution via the new \texttt{mlieBracket\_neg\_right\_apply}. The negation-distribution lemma is itself derived in $\sim 5$ lines from \texttt{mlieBracket\_const\_smul\_right} at $c = (-1 : \mathbb{K})$ plus \texttt{neg\_one\_smul}. The slot-1 sibling \texttt{mlieBracket\_neg\_left\_apply} ships in parallel as a Mathlib-PR-shaped helper.
sentences.quote_normalized: cyclic jacobi follows from one leibniz instance plus three antisymmetry rewrites: outer swap via mliebracket\_swap\_apply; inner swap via section-level mliebracket\_swap; and inner-negation distribution via the new mliebracket\_neg\_right\_apply. the negation-distribution lemma is itself derived in $\sim 5$ lines from mliebracket\_const\_smul\_right at $c = (-1 : \mathbb{k})$ plus neg\_one\_smul. the slot-1 sibling mliebracket\_neg\_left\_apply ships in parallel as a mathlib-pr-shaped helper.
sentences.agent_notes: TN-OK: both pointwise lemmas verified live. Section-level (funext) versions cut at post-wave audit per W8 4a/4b ship memory.

sentences.quote: To our knowledge no proof assistant has previously formalised cyclic Jacobi for the manifold Lie bracket; Mathlib's Leibniz form is the closest precedent.
sentences.quote_normalized: to our knowledge no proof assistant has previously formalised cyclic jacobi for the manifold lie bracket; mathlib's leibniz form is the closest precedent.
sentences.agent_notes: No-prior-art claim per project memory project_phase6f_w8_session4_5_shipped.md.

sentences.quote: For $\mathrm{cov}$ a covariant derivative on the tangent bundle that is torsion-free in the sense of Bonn, and additive in the section argument, the cyclic Bianchi sum vanishes. Three torsion-free regroupings collapse the inner double-derivative pairs into bracket-of-brackets terms; the cyclic sum then reduces to cyclic Jacobi.
sentences.quote_normalized: for $cov$ a covariant derivative on the tangent bundle that is torsion-free in the sense of bonn, and additive in the section argument, the cyclic bianchi sum vanishes. three torsion-free regroupings collapse the inner double-derivative pairs into bracket-of-brackets terms; the cyclic sum then reduces to cyclic jacobi.
sentences.agent_notes: TN-OK: both load-bearing theorems confirmed live.

sentences.quote: We ship two new root-namespace lemmas in Mathlib upstream-style: \texttt{\_root\_.IsCovariantDerivativeOn.neg} (derived from Bonn's $.\texttt{smul\_const}$ at $a = -1$ plus \texttt{neg\_one\_smul}), and \texttt{\_root\_.IsCovariantDerivativeOn.sub}.
sentences.quote_normalized: we ship two new root-namespace lemmas in mathlib upstream-style: \_root\_.iscovariantderivativeon.neg (derived from bonn's $.smul\_const$ at $a = -1$ plus neg\_one\_smul), and \_root\_.iscovariantderivativeon.sub.
sentences.agent_notes: TN-OK: both _root_-namespaced helpers verified at lines 339+365 of BundleRiemann.lean.

sentences.quote: The wave-headline Session 4b deliverable \texttt{bundleRiemannAux\_first\_bianchi\_torsionfree\_of\_isCovariantDerivativeOn} re-expresses the abstract first Bianchi by consuming Bonn's \texttt{IsCovariantDerivativeOn} directly.
sentences.quote_normalized: the wave-headline session 4b deliverable bundleriemannaux\_first\_bianchi\_torsionfree\_of\_iscovariantderivativeon re-expresses the abstract first bianchi by consuming bonn's iscovariantderivativeon directly.
sentences.agent_notes: TN-OK: theorem present in BundleRiemann.lean.

sentences.quote: Three section-level \texttt{ext}/\texttt{funext} lifts of the pointwise lemmas above were retroactively cut at the post-wave audit as P3 plumbing: \texttt{mlieBracket\_neg\_right} / \texttt{mlieBracket\_neg\_left} / \texttt{mlieBracket\_cyclic\_jacobi} each had no internal or external consumer in the SK-EFT project, with substantive content living in the corresponding \texttt{\_apply} versions.
sentences.quote_normalized: three section-level ext/funext lifts of the pointwise lemmas above were retroactively cut at the post-wave audit as p3 plumbing: mliebracket\_neg\_right / mliebracket\_neg\_left / mliebracket\_cyclic\_jacobi each had no internal or external consumer in the sk-eft project, with substantive content living in the corresponding \_apply versions.
sentences.agent_notes: Session 4a+4b strengthening cut documented per project memory project_phase6f_w8_session4_5_shipped.md; matches W8 ship memory's '3 mlieBracket plumbing cuts'.

sentences.quote: The kernel theorem \texttt{leviCivita\_pointwise\_unique\_of\_koszul} takes two abstract connections … plus the hypothesis that for every test section triple $(X, Y, Z)$ at the chosen point $x$, $g_x(\mathrm{cov}\,Y\,x\,(X\,x), Z\,x) = g_x(\mathrm{cov}'\,Y\,x\,(X\,x), Z\,x)$. The non-degeneracy hypothesis is encoded as the auxiliary def \texttt{IsNondegenerateAt}\,$g\,x$.
sentences.quote_normalized: the kernel theorem levicivita\_pointwise\_unique\_of\_koszul takes two abstract connections ... plus the hypothesis that for every test section triple $(x, y, z)$ at the chosen point $x$, $g_x(cov\,y\,x\,(x\,x), z\,x) = g_x(cov'\,y\,x\,(x\,x), z\,x)$. the non-degeneracy hypothesis is encoded as the auxiliary def isnondegenerateat\,$g\,x$.
sentences.agent_notes: TN-OK: both verified live in LeviCivita.lean.

sentences.quote: The Levi-Civita predicate ships as a four-conjunct \texttt{structure} on $(\mathrm{cov}, g)$: is\_cov, torsion\_free, metric\_symm, metric\_compatible.
sentences.quote_normalized: the levi-civita predicate ships as a four-conjunct structure on $(cov, g)$: is\_cov, torsion\_free, metric\_symm, metric\_compatible.
sentences.agent_notes: TN-OK: IsLeviCivita structure live in LeviCivita.lean. Four-conjunct claim verified by W8 Session 5+5b ship memory.

sentences.quote: The metric-compat condition uses Mathlib's $\mathrm{extDerivFun}$ (clean $\mathbb{K}$-valued exterior derivative wrapper) rather than $\mathrm{mfderiv}$ directly.
sentences.quote_normalized: the metric-compat condition uses mathlib's $extderivfun$ (clean $\mathbb{k}$-valued exterior derivative wrapper) rather than $mfderiv$ directly.
sentences.agent_notes: extDerivFun is in pinned Mathlib 8850ed93 (project memory project_phase6f_w8_session4_5_shipped.md confirms substantive 4-conjunct uses Mathlib's extDerivFun).

sentences.quote: Theorem \texttt{koszul\_identity\_of\_isLeviCivita} derives the Koszul bilinear-form identity from \texttt{IsLeviCivita}: $2 \cdot g_x(\mathrm{cov}\,Y\,x\,(X\,x), Z\,x) = \mathrm{extDerivFun}(g(Y,Z))_x(X_x) + \mathrm{extDerivFun}(g(Z,X))_x(Y_x) - \mathrm{extDerivFun}(g(X,Y))_x(Z_x) + g_x([X,Y]_x, Z_x) - g_x([X,Z]_x, Y_x) - g_x([Y,Z]_x, X_x)$. The combined step closes via \texttt{linear\_combination} with derived coefficients $[-1, -1, +1, -1, -1, +1, -1, -1, +1]$ across the nine hypotheses.
sentences.quote_normalized: theorem koszul\_identity\_of\_islevicivita derives the koszul bilinear-form identity from islevicivita: $2 \cdot g_x(cov\,y\,x\,(x\,x), z\,x) = extderivfun(g(y,z))_x(x_x) + extderivfun(g(z,x))_x(y_x) - extderivfun(g(x,y))_x(z_x) + g_x([x,y]_x, z_x) - g_x([x,z]_x, y_x) - g_x([y,z]_x, x_x)$. the combined step closes via linear\_combination with derived coefficients $[-1, -1, +1, -1, -1, +1, -1, -1, +1]$ across the nine hypotheses.
sentences.agent_notes: TN-OK: koszul_identity_of_isLeviCivita verified live; per ship memory uses linear_combination with derived coefficients (Wald §3.1 derivation).

sentences.quote: Composing the Koszul identity (Session 5b) with the algebraic uniqueness kernel (Session 5) gives the full uniqueness: \texttt{leviCivita\_unique\_of\_isLeviCivita}. … The cancellation step uses \texttt{mul\_left\_cancel}\textsubscript{0}.
sentences.quote_normalized: composing the koszul identity (session 5b) with the algebraic uniqueness kernel (session 5) gives the full uniqueness: levicivita\_unique\_of\_islevicivita. ... the cancellation step uses mul\_left\_cancel\textsubscript{0}.
sentences.agent_notes: TN-OK: both targets confirmed. Per ship memory composition routes via `mul_left_cancel₀` for (2 ≠ 0).

sentences.quote: This is the substantive Wald \S 3.1 / Kobayashi-Nomizu IV.2.2 uniqueness theorem at bundle level, deferred from \texttt{RiemannianConnection.lean}'s coordinate-scope predicate-level form (which was a P5 trivial-trans on the def-as-equality $\Gamma = \texttt{christoffelKoszul}$ and was retroactively cut at the W7+W8(S1-3) reflexive strengthening pass).
sentences.quote_normalized: this is the substantive wald \s 3.1 / kobayashi-nomizu iv.2.2 uniqueness theorem at bundle level, deferred from riemannianconnection.lean's coordinate-scope predicate-level form (which was a p5 trivial-trans on the def-as-equality $\gamma = christoffelkoszul$ and was retroactively cut at the w7+w8(s1-3) reflexive strengthening pass).
